LabelThe approximate average duration of time between when samples reach the lab and when they are concentrated (if concentrated)
Datatypestring
Description

The pre_conc_storage_time variable records response to the prompt, "The approximate average duration of time between when samples reach the lab and when they are concentrated (if concentrated)".
706) pre_conc_storage_temp ¶
LabelThe storage temperature of samples after reaching the lab and prior to concentration (if concentrated)
Datatypestring
Description

The pre_conc_storage_temp variable records response to the prompt, "The storage temperature of samples after reaching the lab and prior to concentration (if concentrated)".
707) pre_ext_storage_time ¶
LabelThe approximate average duration of time between when samples are concentrated (if concentrated) and when they are extracted
Datatypestring
Description

The pre_ext_storage_time variable records response to the prompt, "The approximate average duration of time between when samples are concentrated (if concentrated) and when they are extracted".
708) pre_ext_storage_temp ¶
LabelThe storage temperature of samples after concentration (if concentrated) and prior to extraction
Datatypestring
Description

The pre_ext_storage_temp variable records response to the prompt, "The storage temperature of samples after concentration (if concentrated) and prior to extraction".
709) tot_conc_vol ¶
LabelTotal volume of sample concentrated (if concentrated); this total volume is not necessarily assayed and is not necessarily equal to the value specified in 'equiv_sewage_amt' ([float, greater than or equal to 0, unit: mL]; [empty])
Datatypestring
Description

The tot_conc_vol variable records response to the prompt, "Total volume of sample concentrated (if concentrated); this total volume is not necessarily assayed and is not necessarily equal to the value specified in 'equiv_sewage_amt' ([float, greater than or equal to 0, unit: mL]; [empty])".
710) ext_blank ¶
LabelAre extraction blanks included in the extraction process?
Datatypestring
Description

The ext_blank variable records response to the prompt, "Are extraction blanks included in the extraction process?".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 2 permissible string values.

Enumeration
Yes Yes
No No
711) rec_eff_target_name ¶
LabelName of the recovery efficiency control target that is spiked in (If 'rec_eff_percent' is equal to a value other than "-1", then this must have a non-empty value. Added heat inactivated SARS-CoV-2 virus)
Datatypestring
Description

The rec_eff_target_name variable records response to the prompt, "Name of the recovery efficiency control target that is spiked in (If 'rec_eff_percent' is equal to a value other than "-1", then this must have a non-empty value. Added heat inactivated SARS-CoV-2 virus)".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 9 permissible string values.

Enumeration
bcov vaccine bcov vaccine
brsv vaccine brsv vaccine
murine coronavirus murine coronavirus
oc43 oc43
phi6 phi6
puro puro
ms2 coliphage ms2 coliphage
hep g armored rna hep g armored rna
heat inactivated SARS-CoV-2 virus heat inactivated SARS-CoV-2 virus
712) rec_eff_spike_matrix ¶
LabelMatrix into which the recovery efficiency control target is spiked (If 'rec_eff_target_name' has a non-empty value, then this must have a non-empty value)
Datatypestring
Description

The rec_eff_spike_matrix variable records response to the prompt, "Matrix into which the recovery efficiency control target is spiked (If 'rec_eff_target_name' has a non-empty value, then this must have a non-empty value)".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 5 permissible string values.

Enumeration
raw sample raw sample
raw sample post pasteurization raw sample post pasteurization
clarified sample clarified sample
sample concentrate sample concentrate
lysis buffer lysis buffer
713) rec_eff_spike_conc ¶
LabelSpike concentration, on average, of the recovery control on a per sample volume basis (If 'rec_eff_target_name' has a non-empty value, then this must have a non-empty value)
Datatypestring
Description

The rec_eff_spike_conc variable records response to the prompt, "Spike concentration, on average, of the recovery control on a per sample volume basis (If 'rec_eff_target_name' has a non-empty value, then this must have a non-empty value)".
714) pasteurized ¶
LabelWas the sample pasteurized?
Datatypestring
Description

The pasteurized variable records response to the prompt, "Was the sample pasteurized?".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 2 permissible string values.

Enumeration
Yes Yes
No No

SARSCoV2 Quantification Method32 elements

715) pcr_target ¶
LabelThe PCR gene target used to quantify SARS-CoV-2 (Added taqpath n1)
Datatypestring
Description

The pcr_target variable records response to the prompt, "The PCR gene target used to quantify SARS-CoV-2 (Added taqpath n1)".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 15 permissible string values.

Enumeration
n1 n1
n2 n2
n3 n3
e_sarbeco e_sarbeco
n_sarbeco n_sarbeco
rdrp_sarsr rdrp_sarsr
niid_2019-ncov_n niid_2019-ncov_n
rdrp gene / ncov_ip2 rdrp gene / ncov_ip2
rdrp gene / ncov_ip4 rdrp gene / ncov_ip4
taqpath n taqpath n
taqpath n1 taqpath n1
taqpath s taqpath s
orf1b orf1b
orf1ab orf1ab
n1 and n2 combined n1 and n2 combined
716) pcr_target_ref ¶
LabelA publication, website, or brief description of the PCR gene target used
Datatypestring
Description

The pcr_target_ref variable records response to the prompt, "A publication, website, or brief description of the PCR gene target used".
717) lod_ref ¶
LabelA publication, website, or brief description of the method used to calculate the limit of detection
Datatypestring
Description

The lod_ref variable records response to the prompt, "A publication, website, or brief description of the method used to calculate the limit of detection".
718) hum_frac_target_mic ¶
LabelName of microbial target used to estimate human fecal content
Datatypestring
Description

The hum_frac_target_mic variable records response to the prompt, "Name of microbial target used to estimate human fecal content".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 3 permissible string values.

Enumeration
pepper mild mottle virus pepper mild mottle virus
crassphage crassphage
hf183 hf183
719) hum_frac_target_mic_ref ¶
LabelA publication, website, or brief description of the microbial target specified in 'hum_frac_target_mic'
Datatypestring
Description

The hum_frac_target_mic_ref variable records response to the prompt, "A publication, website, or brief description of the microbial target specified in 'hum_frac_target_mic'".
720) hum_frac_target_chem ¶
LabelName of chemical compound used to estimate human fecal content
Datatypestring
Description

The hum_frac_target_chem variable records response to the prompt, "Name of chemical compound used to estimate human fecal content".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 4 permissible string values.

Enumeration
caffeine caffeine
creatinine creatinine
sucralose sucralose
ibuprofen ibuprofen
721) hum_frac_target_chem_ref ¶
LabelA publication, website, or brief description of the chemical compound specified in 'hum_frac_target_chem'
Datatypestring
Description

The hum_frac_target_chem_ref variable records response to the prompt, "A publication, website, or brief description of the chemical compound specified in 'hum_frac_target_chem'".
722) other_norm_name ¶
LabelName of a target or compound not specified in 'hum_frac_target_mic' or 'hum_frac_target_chem' used to estimate human fecal content
Datatypestring
Description

The other_norm_name variable records response to the prompt, "Name of a target or compound not specified in 'hum_frac_target_mic' or 'hum_frac_target_chem' used to estimate human fecal content".
723) other_norm_ref ¶
LabelA publication, website, or brief description of the target or compound specified in 'other_norm_name'
Datatypestring
Description

The other_norm_ref variable records response to the prompt, "A publication, website, or brief description of the target or compound specified in 'other_norm_name'".
724) quant_stan_type ¶
LabelThe type of nucleic acid used as a standard for SARS-CoV-2 quantification
Datatypestring
Description

The quant_stan_type variable records response to the prompt, "The type of nucleic acid used as a standard for SARS-CoV-2 quantification".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 2 permissible string values.

Enumeration
DNA DNA
RNA RNA
725) test_result_date ¶
LabelThe date on which this SARS-CoV-2 measurement was made (Lab result date)
Datatypestring
Description

The test_result_date variable records response to the prompt, "The date on which this SARS-CoV-2 measurement was made (Lab result date)".
726) sars_cov2_units ¶
LabelUnits of SARS-CoV-2 sample concentration (Added copies/mL wastewater)
Datatypestring
Description

The sars_cov2_units variable records response to the prompt, "Units of SARS-CoV-2 sample concentration (Added copies/mL wastewater)".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 7 permissible string values.

Enumeration
copies/L wastewater copies/L wastewater
copies/mL wastewater copies/mL wastewater
log10 copies/L wastewater log10 copies/L wastewater
copies/g wet sludge copies/g wet sludge
log10 copies/g wet sludge log10 copies/g wet sludge
copies/g dry sludge copies/g dry sludge
log10 copies/g dry sludge log10 copies/g dry sludge
727) sars_cov2_avg_conc ¶
LabelConcentration of SARS-CoV-2 back-calculated to unconcentrated sample basis; enter "0" if no amplification occurred, using the definition of amplification described in 'ntc_amplify'; otherwise, enter the estimated concentration; do not adjust for matrix recovery efficiency ([any float other than 0]; 0 (if no amplification observed) [units specified in 'sars_cov2_units'])
Datatypestring
Description

The sars_cov2_avg_conc variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration of SARS-CoV-2 back-calculated to unconcentrated sample basis; enter "0" if no amplification occurred, using the definition of amplification described in 'ntc_amplify'; otherwise, enter the estimated concentration; do not adjust for matrix recovery efficiency ([any float other than 0]; 0 (if no amplification observed) [units specified in 'sars_cov2_units'])".
728) sars_cov2_std_error ¶
LabelStandard error (SE) of SARS-CoV-2 in wastewater sample, or best estimate that is consistently available. If sample replicates are always performed, use SE of sample replicates; else, if processing replicates are always performed, use SE of processing replicates; else, if qPCR is performed, use SE of PCR replicates; else, if digital PCR is performed, use error from multiple replicates if available, and Poisson error if not ([greater than or equal to 0]; -1 (if cannot be calculated, such as when no amplification observed); [empty][units specified in 'sars_cov2_units'])
Datatypestring
Description

The sars_cov2_std_error variable records response to the prompt, "Standard error (SE) of SARS-CoV-2 in wastewater sample, or best estimate that is consistently available. If sample replicates are always performed, use SE of sample replicates; else, if processing replicates are always performed, use SE of processing replicates; else, if qPCR is performed, use SE of PCR replicates; else, if digital PCR is performed, use error from multiple replicates if available, and Poisson error if not ([greater than or equal to 0]; -1 (if cannot be calculated, such as when no amplification observed); [empty][units specified in 'sars_cov2_units'])".
729) sars_cov2_cl_95_lo ¶
LabelLower bound of 95% confidence interval of SARS-CoV-2 in wastewater sample, or best estimate that is consistently available. Follow the same hierarchy as described for standard error. (Note: 'cl' stands for confidence limit) ([any float other than -1]; -1 (if cannot be calculated, such as when no amplification observed); [empty][units specified in 'sars_cov2_units'])
Datatypestring
Description

The sars_cov2_cl_95_lo variable records response to the prompt, "Lower bound of 95% confidence interval of SARS-CoV-2 in wastewater sample, or best estimate that is consistently available. Follow the same hierarchy as described for standard error. (Note: 'cl' stands for confidence limit) ([any float other than -1]; -1 (if cannot be calculated, such as when no amplification observed); [empty][units specified in 'sars_cov2_units'])".
730) sars_cov2_cl_95_up ¶
LabelUpper bound of 95% confidence interval of SARS-CoV-2 in wastewater sample, or best estimate that is consistently available. Follow the same hierarchy as described for standard error. (Note: 'cl' stands for confidence limit) ([any float other than -1]; -1 (if cannot be calculated, such as when no amplification observed); [empty][units specified in 'sars_cov2_units'])
Datatypestring
Description

The sars_cov2_cl_95_up variable records response to the prompt, "Upper bound of 95% confidence interval of SARS-CoV-2 in wastewater sample, or best estimate that is consistently available. Follow the same hierarchy as described for standard error. (Note: 'cl' stands for confidence limit) ([any float other than -1]; -1 (if cannot be calculated, such as when no amplification observed); [empty][units specified in 'sars_cov2_units'])".
731) sars_cov2_below_lod ¶
LabelWas the concentration of SARS-CoV-2 below the limit of detection?
Datatypestring
Description

The sars_cov2_below_lod variable records response to the prompt, "Was the concentration of SARS-CoV-2 below the limit of detection?".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 2 permissible string values.

Enumeration
Yes Yes
No No
732) lod_sewage ¶
LabelSARS-CoV-2 limit of detection back-calculated to unconcentrated sample basis ([units specified in 'sars_cov2_units'])
Datatypestring
Description

The lod_sewage variable records response to the prompt, "SARS-CoV-2 limit of detection back-calculated to unconcentrated sample basis ([units specified in 'sars_cov2_units'])".
733) ntc_amplify ¶
LabelFor qPCR, did any no-template controls on this instrument run have a Ct value less than 40? For ddPCR, did any no-template controls on this instrument run have 3 or more positive droplets?
Datatypestring
Description

The ntc_amplify variable records response to the prompt, "For qPCR, did any no-template controls on this instrument run have a Ct value less than 40? For ddPCR, did any no-template controls on this instrument run have 3 or more positive droplets?".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 2 permissible string values.

Enumeration
Yes Yes
No No
734) rec_eff_percent ¶
LabelPercent of spiked recovery control, specified in 'rec_eff_target_name', that was recovered ([float, greater than or equal to 0]; -1 (if not tested))
Datatypestring
Description

The rec_eff_percent variable records response to the prompt, "Percent of spiked recovery control, specified in 'rec_eff_target_name', that was recovered ([float, greater than or equal to 0]; -1 (if not tested))".
735) inhibition_detect ¶
LabelWas molecular inhibition detected?
Datatypestring
Description

The inhibition_detect variable records response to the prompt, "Was molecular inhibition detected?".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 3 permissible string values.

Enumeration
Yes Yes
No No
Not tested Not tested
736) inhibition_adjust ¶
LabelWas inhibition incorporated into the SARS-CoV-2 concentration calculation?
Datatypestring
Description

The inhibition_adjust variable records response to the prompt, "Was inhibition incorporated into the SARS-CoV-2 concentration calculation?".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 2 permissible string values.

Enumeration
Yes Yes
No No
737) hum_frac_mic_conc ¶
LabelConcentration of microbial target specified in 'hum_frac_target_mic'; follow the same guidelines outline for 'sars_cov2_avg_conc' ([units specified in 'hum_frac_mic_unit'])
Datatypestring
Description

The hum_frac_mic_conc variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration of microbial target specified in 'hum_frac_target_mic'; follow the same guidelines outline for 'sars_cov2_avg_conc' ([units specified in 'hum_frac_mic_unit'])".
738) hum_frac_mic_unit ¶
LabelConcentration units of microbial target specified in 'hum_frac_target_mic'
Datatypestring
Description

The hum_frac_mic_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration units of microbial target specified in 'hum_frac_target_mic'".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 6 permissible string values.

Enumeration
copies/L wastewater copies/L wastewater
log10 copies/L wastewater log10 copies/L wastewater
copies/g wet sludge copies/g wet sludge
log10 copies/g wet sludge log10 copies/g wet sludge
copies/g dry sludge copies/g dry sludge
log10 copies/g dry sludge log10 copies/g dry sludge
739) hum_frac_chem_conc ¶
LabelConcentration of chemical target specified in 'hum_frac_target_chem' ([units specified in 'hum_frac_chem_unit'])
Datatypestring
Description

The hum_frac_chem_conc variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration of chemical target specified in 'hum_frac_target_chem' ([units specified in 'hum_frac_chem_unit'])".
740) hum_frac_chem_unit ¶
LabelConcentration units of chemical target specified in 'hum_frac_target_chem'
Datatypestring
Description

The hum_frac_chem_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration units of chemical target specified in 'hum_frac_target_chem'".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 6 permissible string values.

Enumeration
micrograms/L wastewater micrograms/L wastewater
log10 micrograms/L wastewater log10 micrograms/L wastewater
micrograms/g wet sludge micrograms/g wet sludge
log10 micrograms/g wet sludge log10 micrograms/g wet sludge
micrograms/g dry sludge micrograms/g dry sludge
log10 micrograms/g dry sludge log10 micrograms/g dry sludge
741) other_norm_conc ¶
LabelConcentration of target spcified in 'other_norm_name' ([units specified in 'other_norm_conc'])
Datatypestring
Description

The other_norm_conc variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration of target spcified in 'other_norm_name' ([units specified in 'other_norm_conc'])".
742) other_norm_unit ¶
LabelConcentration units of target spcified in 'other_norm_name'
Datatypestring
Description

The other_norm_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration units of target spcified in 'other_norm_name'".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 12 permissible string values.

Enumeration
copies/L wastewater copies/L wastewater
log10 copies/L wastewater log10 copies/L wastewater
copies/g wet sludge copies/g wet sludge
log10 copies/g wet sludge log10 copies/g wet sludge
copies/g dry sludge copies/g dry sludge
log10 copies/g dry sludge log10 copies/g dry sludge
micrograms/L wastewater micrograms/L wastewater
log10 micrograms/L wastewater log10 micrograms/L wastewater
micrograms/g wet sludge micrograms/g wet sludge
log10 micrograms/g wet sludge log10 micrograms/g wet sludge
micrograms/g dry sludge micrograms/g dry sludge
log10 micrograms/g dry sludge log10 micrograms/g dry sludge
743) quality_flag ¶
LabelDoes this observation have quality control issues?
Datatypestring
Description

The quality_flag variable records response to the prompt, "Does this observation have quality control issues?".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 2 permissible string values.

Enumeration
Yes Yes
No No
744) stan_ref ¶
LabelA publication, website, or brief description of the quantitative standard material used
Datatypestring
Description

The stan_ref variable records response to the prompt, "A publication, website, or brief description of the quantitative standard material used".
745) inhibition_method ¶
LabelA publication, website, or brief description of the method used to evaluate molecular inhibition
Datatypestring
Description

The inhibition_method variable records response to the prompt, "A publication, website, or brief description of the method used to evaluate molecular inhibition".
746) num_no_target_control ¶
LabelNumber of no-template controls (NTC) per instrument run
Datatypeinteger
Description

The num_no_target_control variable records response to the prompt, "Number of no-template controls (NTC) per instrument run".

Values for this variable are integers that are restricted to the list of 4 permissible integer values.

Enumeration
0 0
1 1
2 2
more than 3 more than 3

Assay Results8 elements

747) specimen_type ¶
LabelSpecimen type used in assay (example: saliva)
Datatypestring
Description

The specimen_type variable records response to the prompt, "Specimen type used in assay (example: saliva)".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 7 permissible string values.

Enumeration
breath breath
hand odor hand odor
plasma plasma
saliva saliva
skin skin
serum serum
exhaled breath condensate exhaled breath condensate
748) specimen_dilution_factor ¶
LabelDilution of specimen before test. The dilution factor is the ratio of the final volume divided by the original volume. (example: 10 (fold))
Datatypestring
Description

The specimen_dilution_factor variable records response to the prompt, "Dilution of specimen before test. The dilution factor is the ratio of the final volume divided by the original volume. (example: 10 (fold))".
749) assay_result ¶
LabelMeasured result. If other, specify result in assay_result_other (example: positive)
Datatypestring
Description

The assay_result variable records response to the prompt, "Measured result. If other, specify result in assay_result_other (example: positive)".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 5 permissible string values.

Enumeration
positive positive
negative negative
failed failed
lost lost
other other
750) assay_result_other ¶
LabelDescription of other assay result
Datatypestring
Description

The assay_result_other variable records response to the prompt, "Description of other assay result".
751) assay_readout ¶
LabelNumerical assay readout (example: 0.7)
Datatypestring
Description

The assay_readout variable records response to the prompt, "Numerical assay readout (example: 0.7)".
752) assay_readout_error ¶
LabelError in assay readout (example: 0.1)
Datatypestring
Description

The assay_readout_error variable records response to the prompt, "Error in assay readout (example: 0.1)".
753) assay_readout_unit ¶
LabelUnit of assay_readout (example: F)
Datatypestring
Description

The assay_readout_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of assay_readout (example: F)".
754) assay_readout_description ¶
LabelDescription of assay readout type (example: capacitance)
Datatypestring
Description

The assay_readout_description variable records response to the prompt, "Description of assay readout type (example: capacitance)".

Results/VOC29 elements

755) voc_compound_name ¶
LabelName of compound/metabolite detected (example: Acetone)
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_compound_name variable records response to the prompt, "Name of compound/metabolite detected (example: Acetone)".
756) voc_compound_formula ¶
LabelChemical formula of compound (example: C3H6O)
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_compound_formula variable records response to the prompt, "Chemical formula of compound (example: C3H6O)".
757) voc_compound_inchikey ¶
LabelInChIKey of compound (unique identifier for data linking) (example: CSCPPACGZOOCGX-UHFFFAOYSA-N)
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_compound_inchikey variable records response to the prompt, "InChIKey of compound (unique identifier for data linking) (example: CSCPPACGZOOCGX-UHFFFAOYSA-N)".
758) voc_compound_concentration ¶
LabelConcentration of compound
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_compound_concentration variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration of compound".
759) voc_compound_concentration_unit ¶
LabelUnit of voc_compound_concentration
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_compound_concentration_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of voc_compound_concentration".
760) voc_compound_retention_time ¶
LabelRetention time for a specific compound
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_compound_retention_time variable records response to the prompt, "Retention time for a specific compound".
761) voc_compound_retention_time_unit ¶
LabelUnit of voc compound retention time (example: s)
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_compound_retention_time_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of voc compound retention time (example: s)".
762) voc_compound_secondary_retention_time ¶
LabelRetention time for the second peak used if applicable
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_compound_secondary_retention_time variable records response to the prompt, "Retention time for the second peak used if applicable".
763) voc_compound_retention_time_description ¶
LabelDecription of retention times used in calculation of data (one peak or two peaks)
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_compound_retention_time_description variable records response to the prompt, "Decription of retention times used in calculation of data (one peak or two peaks)".
764) voc_compound_detail ¶
LabelComments about the detection of compound (example: probable compound based on retention time)
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_compound_detail variable records response to the prompt, "Comments about the detection of compound (example: probable compound based on retention time)".
765) voc_relative_abundance ¶
LabelThe relative abundance of a VOC in a sample.
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_relative_abundance variable records response to the prompt, "The relative abundance of a VOC in a sample.".
766) voc_abundance_ratio_ir ¶
LabelRatio of peak area of listed analyte to peak area of listed internal reference
Datatypestring
Description

The voc_abundance_ratio_ir variable records response to the prompt, "Ratio of peak area of listed analyte to peak area of listed internal reference".
767) internal_reference ¶
LabelSpecific internal reference used for peak area ratios
Datatypestring
Description

The internal_reference variable records response to the prompt, "Specific internal reference used for peak area ratios".
768) internal_reference_description ¶
LabelDescription of internal reference used
Datatypestring
Description

The internal_reference_description variable records response to the prompt, "Description of internal reference used".
769) peak_id ¶
LabelUnique identifer for the peak in a mass spectrum defined by the mass and retention time. (example: peak_1)
Datatypestring
Description

The peak_id variable records response to the prompt, "Unique identifer for the peak in a mass spectrum defined by the mass and retention time. (example: peak_1)".
770) peak_mass ¶
LabelThe most probable mass of the peak after the identification or mass-to-charge ratio (m/z) when not identified. (g/mol when peak identified and m/z when peak not identified . Example: 57.1)
Datatypestring
Description

The peak_mass variable records response to the prompt, "The most probable mass of the peak after the identification or mass-to-charge ratio (m/z) when not identified. (g/mol when peak identified and m/z when peak not identified . Example: 57.1)".
771) peak_mass_unit ¶
LabelUnit of peak_mass (example: m/z)
Datatypestring
Description

The peak_mass_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of peak_mass (example: m/z)".
772) peak_retention_time ¶
LabelThe measure of time taken for a peak to pass through a chromatography column. (example: 22.1)
Datatypestring
Description

The peak_retention_time variable records response to the prompt, "The measure of time taken for a peak to pass through a chromatography column. (example: 22.1)".
773) peak_retention_time_unit ¶
LabelUnit of peak_retention_time (example: min)
Datatypestring
Description

The peak_retention_time_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of peak_retention_time (example: min)".
778) signal_intensity ¶
LabelMeasured signal intensity (example: signal intensity in a chromatogram)
Datatypestring
Description

The signal_intensity variable records response to the prompt, "Measured signal intensity (example: signal intensity in a chromatogram)".
779) gc_amplitude ¶
LabelThe signal generated from the ionization of VOC seprated compounds
Datatypestring
Description

The gc_amplitude variable records response to the prompt, "The signal generated from the ionization of VOC seprated compounds".
780) gc_elution_time ¶
LabelThe time needed for a certain compound to pass through the GC column
Datatypestring
Description

The gc_elution_time variable records response to the prompt, "The time needed for a certain compound to pass through the GC column".
781) gc_elution_time_unit ¶
LabelUnit of gc_elution_time
Datatypestring
Description

The gc_elution_time_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of gc_elution_time".
782) analyte_binding_peptide_name ¶
LabelName of a peptide probe that binds a VOC analyte
Datatypestring
Description

The analyte_binding_peptide_name variable records response to the prompt, "Name of a peptide probe that binds a VOC analyte".
783) analyte_binding_peptide_sequence ¶
LabelSequence of the analyte binding peptide
Datatypestring
Description

The analyte_binding_peptide_sequence variable records response to the prompt, "Sequence of the analyte binding peptide".
784) analyte_binding_peptide_parent_protein_id ¶
LabelOdorant binding protein from which the peptide sequence was derived
Datatypestring
Description

The analyte_binding_peptide_parent_protein_id variable records response to the prompt, "Odorant binding protein from which the peptide sequence was derived".
785) analyte_binding_peptide_parent_protein_url ¶
LabelURL of the odorant binding protein in the iOBPdb database
Datatypestring
Description

The analyte_binding_peptide_parent_protein_url variable records response to the prompt, "URL of the odorant binding protein in the iOBPdb database".
786) analyte_binding_peptide_reference_protein_id ¶
LabelOdorant binding protein homolog with 3D structural information
Datatypestring
Description

The analyte_binding_peptide_reference_protein_id variable records response to the prompt, "Odorant binding protein homolog with 3D structural information".
787) analyte_binding_peptide_reference_structure_id ¶
LabelProtein data bank id of the reference protein structure
Datatypestring
Description

The analyte_binding_peptide_reference_structure_id variable records response to the prompt, "Protein data bank id of the reference protein structure".

Results/GCMS/LCMS4 elements

774) retention_time ¶
LabelRetention time
Datatypestring
Description

The retention_time variable records response to the prompt, "Retention time".
775) retention_time_unit ¶
LabelUnit of retention_time (example: s)
Datatypestring
Description

The retention_time_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of retention_time (example: s)".
776) identified_compound_name ¶
LabelName of the compound identified by GCMS or LCMS (example: Acetone)
Datatypestring
Description

The identified_compound_name variable records response to the prompt, "Name of the compound identified by GCMS or LCMS (example: Acetone)".
777) identified_compound_inchikey ¶
LabelInChIKey of the compound identified by GCMS or LCMS (example: CSCPPACGZOOCGX-UHFFFAOYSA-N)
Datatypestring
Description

The identified_compound_inchikey variable records response to the prompt, "InChIKey of the compound identified by GCMS or LCMS (example: CSCPPACGZOOCGX-UHFFFAOYSA-N)".

Analytical Performance48 elements

788) sample_size ¶
LabelNumber of samples in a sample_group used to calculate metrics
Datatypestring
Description

The sample_size variable records response to the prompt, "Number of samples in a sample_group used to calculate metrics".
789) p_value ¶
LabelThe p-value is the probability of obtaining test results at least as extreme as the result actually observed, under the assumption that the null hypothesis is correct.
Datatypestring
Description

The p_value variable records response to the prompt, "The p-value is the probability of obtaining test results at least as extreme as the result actually observed, under the assumption that the null hypothesis is correct.".
790) p_value_description ¶
LabelDescription how to interpret the p-value for the specific measurement.
Datatypestring
Description

The p_value_description variable records response to the prompt, "Description how to interpret the p-value for the specific measurement.".
791) limit_of_blank ¶
LabelLimit of Blank (LOB). The highest apparent analyte concentration expected to be found when a replicate of a sample containing no analyte are tested. (Armbruster DA, Pry T. Limit of blank, limit of detection and limit of quantitation. Clin Biochem Rev. 2008 Aug;29 Suppl 1(Suppl 1):S49-52. PMID: 18852857; PMCID: PMC2556583.)
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_blank variable records response to the prompt, "Limit of Blank (LOB). The highest apparent analyte concentration expected to be found when a replicate of a sample containing no analyte are tested. (Armbruster DA, Pry T. Limit of blank, limit of detection and limit of quantitation. Clin Biochem Rev. 2008 Aug;29 Suppl 1(Suppl 1):S49-52. PMID: 18852857; PMCID: PMC2556583.)".
792) limit_of_blank_unit ¶
LabelConcentration unit for limit_of_blank
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_blank_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration unit for limit_of_blank".
793) limit_of_detection ¶
LabelLimit of Detection (LOD). The minimum amount or concentration of analyte that can be reliably distinguished from zero. (Armbruster DA, Pry T. Limit of blank, limit of detection and limit of quantitation. Clin Biochem Rev. 2008 Aug;29 Suppl 1(Suppl 1):S49-52. PMID: 18852857; PMCID: PMC2556583.)
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_detection variable records response to the prompt, "Limit of Detection (LOD). The minimum amount or concentration of analyte that can be reliably distinguished from zero. (Armbruster DA, Pry T. Limit of blank, limit of detection and limit of quantitation. Clin Biochem Rev. 2008 Aug;29 Suppl 1(Suppl 1):S49-52. PMID: 18852857; PMCID: PMC2556583.)".
794) limit_of_detection_unit ¶
LabelConcentration unit for limit_of_detection
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_detection_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration unit for limit_of_detection".
795) limit_of_detection_reference_gene ¶
LabelName of the gene used to define the concentration of a viral sample (Example: ORF1a)
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_detection_reference_gene variable records response to the prompt, "Name of the gene used to define the concentration of a viral sample (Example: ORF1a)".
796) limit_of_detection_sn_ratio ¶
LabelSignal to noise ratio used to define the limit of detection in standard deviations. (Example: 3)
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_detection_sn_ratio variable records response to the prompt, "Signal to noise ratio used to define the limit of detection in standard deviations. (Example: 3)".
797) limit_of_detection_detail ¶
LabelDescription of how limit of detection was determined. (Example: A dilution series of heat inactivated SARS-CoV-2 virus stock (USA-WA1/2020) was prepared in natural clinical matrix (pooled nasopharyngeal swab samples eluted in VTM). Five (5) replicates were tested per dilution. The testing was performed as per the recommended instructions for use, with the virus in clinical matrix applied directly onto the Swab until the saturation of the flocked tip. The estimated LoD concentration for verification was selected as the concentration in between the dilution factor that returned 5/5 positive test outcomes (100%) and the next dilution factor that returned 0/5 positive (0%) based on the qualitative result. Source: https://www.fda.gov/media/144592/download.)
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_detection_detail variable records response to the prompt, "Description of how limit of detection was determined. (Example: A dilution series of heat inactivated SARS-CoV-2 virus stock (USA-WA1/2020) was prepared in natural clinical matrix (pooled nasopharyngeal swab samples eluted in VTM). Five (5) replicates were tested per dilution. The testing was performed as per the recommended instructions for use, with the virus in clinical matrix applied directly onto the Swab until the saturation of the flocked tip. The estimated LoD concentration for verification was selected as the concentration in between the dilution factor that returned 5/5 positive test outcomes (100%) and the next dilution factor that returned 0/5 positive (0%) based on the qualitative result. Source: https://www.fda.gov/media/144592/download.)".
798) limit_of_detection_ci_percent ¶
LabelPercent used to define two-sided confidence interval for limit_of_detection. (Example: 95)
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_detection_ci_percent variable records response to the prompt, "Percent used to define two-sided confidence interval for limit_of_detection. (Example: 95)".
799) limit_of_detection_ci_min ¶
LabelLower bound of confidence interval for limit_of_detection.
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_detection_ci_min variable records response to the prompt, "Lower bound of confidence interval for limit_of_detection.".
800) limit_of_detection_ci_max ¶
LabelUpper bound of confidence interval for limit_of_detection.
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_detection_ci_max variable records response to the prompt, "Upper bound of confidence interval for limit_of_detection.".
801) limit_of_quantitation ¶
LabelLimit of Quantiation (LOQ). The minimum amount or concentration of analyte in the test sample that can be quantified with acceptable precision. (Armbruster DA, Pry T. Limit of blank, limit of detection and limit of quantitation. Clin Biochem Rev. 2008 Aug;29 Suppl 1(Suppl 1):S49-52. PMID: 18852857; PMCID: PMC2556583.)
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_quantitation variable records response to the prompt, "Limit of Quantiation (LOQ). The minimum amount or concentration of analyte in the test sample that can be quantified with acceptable precision. (Armbruster DA, Pry T. Limit of blank, limit of detection and limit of quantitation. Clin Biochem Rev. 2008 Aug;29 Suppl 1(Suppl 1):S49-52. PMID: 18852857; PMCID: PMC2556583.)".
802) limit_of_quantitation_unit ¶
LabelConcentration unit for limit_of_quantitation
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_quantitation_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration unit for limit_of_quantitation".
803) limit_of_quantitation_sn_ratio ¶
LabelSignal to noise ratio used to define the limit of quantification in standard deviations. (Example: 5)
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_quantitation_sn_ratio variable records response to the prompt, "Signal to noise ratio used to define the limit of quantification in standard deviations. (Example: 5)".
804) limit_of_quantitation_detail ¶
LabelDescription of how limit of quantition was determined. (COVID-19 Home Test was tested with up to a concentration of 106.43 TCID 50 /mL of heat inactivated SARS-CoV-2 virus (USA-WA1/2020 Isolate). Source: https://www.fda.gov/media/144592/download.)
Datatypestring
Description

The limit_of_quantitation_detail variable records response to the prompt, "Description of how limit of quantition was determined. (COVID-19 Home Test was tested with up to a concentration of 106.43 TCID 50 /mL of heat inactivated SARS-CoV-2 virus (USA-WA1/2020 Isolate). Source: https://www.fda.gov/media/144592/download.)".
805) dynamic_range_min ¶
LabelDynamic range min is the lowest concentration of an analyte that can be reliably detected by the assay.
Datatypestring
Description

The dynamic_range_min variable records response to the prompt, "Dynamic range min is the lowest concentration of an analyte that can be reliably detected by the assay.".
806) dynamic_range_min_unit ¶
LabelUnit of dynamic_range_min
Datatypestring
Description

The dynamic_range_min_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of dynamic_range_min".
807) dynamic_range_max ¶
LabelDynamic range ax is the heighest concentration of an analyte that can be reliably detected by the assay.
Datatypestring
Description

The dynamic_range_max variable records response to the prompt, "Dynamic range ax is the heighest concentration of an analyte that can be reliably detected by the assay.".
808) dynamic_range_max_unit ¶
LabelUnit of dynamic_range_max
Datatypestring
Description

The dynamic_range_max_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of dynamic_range_max".
809) high_dose_hook_effect ¶
LabelDescription of high dose hook effect. (Example: No high dose hook effect was observed when the Ellume COVID-19 Home Test was tested with up to a concentration of 106.43 TCID 50 /mL of heat inactivated SARS-CoV-2 virus (USA-WA1/2020 Isolate). Source: https://www.fda.gov/media/144592/download.)
Datatypestring
Description

The high_dose_hook_effect variable records response to the prompt, "Description of high dose hook effect. (Example: No high dose hook effect was observed when the Ellume COVID-19 Home Test was tested with up to a concentration of 106.43 TCID 50 /mL of heat inactivated SARS-CoV-2 virus (USA-WA1/2020 Isolate). Source: https://www.fda.gov/media/144592/download.)".
810) non_clinical_cutoff_min ¶
LabelTest is considered positive if the assay readout is greater than or equal the non_clinical_cutoff_min value and negative if the assay readout is less than the non_clinical_cutoff_min value, e.g., cutoff value chosen from the ROC curve.
Datatypestring
Description

The non_clinical_cutoff_min variable records response to the prompt, "Test is considered positive if the assay readout is greater than or equal the non_clinical_cutoff_min value and negative if the assay readout is less than the non_clinical_cutoff_min value, e.g., cutoff value chosen from the ROC curve.".
811) non_clinical_cutoff_min_unit ¶
LabelUnit of non_clinical_cutoff_min
Datatypestring
Description

The non_clinical_cutoff_min_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of non_clinical_cutoff_min".
812) non_clinical_sensitivity ¶
LabelThe proportion of system-based positives; calculated as 100xTP/(TP+FN). Used for comparison with a VQA standard.
Datatypestring
Description

The non_clinical_sensitivity variable records response to the prompt, "The proportion of system-based positives; calculated as 100xTP/(TP+FN). Used for comparison with a VQA standard.".
813) non_clinical_sensitivity_ci_percent ¶
LabelPercent used to define two-sided confidence interval for non_clinical sensitivity. (Example: 95)
Datatypestring
Description

The non_clinical_sensitivity_ci_percent variable records response to the prompt, "Percent used to define two-sided confidence interval for non_clinical sensitivity. (Example: 95)".
814) non_clinical_sensitivity_ci_min ¶
LabelLower bound of confidence interval for non_clinical sensitivity.
Datatypestring
Description

The non_clinical_sensitivity_ci_min variable records response to the prompt, "Lower bound of confidence interval for non_clinical sensitivity.".
815) non_clinical_sensitivity_ci_max ¶
LabelUpper bound of confidence interval for non_clinical sensitivity.
Datatypestring
Description

The non_clinical_sensitivity_ci_max variable records response to the prompt, "Upper bound of confidence interval for non_clinical sensitivity.".
816) non_clinical_specificity ¶
LabelThe proportion of system-based negatives; calculated as 100xTN/(TN+FP). Used for comparison with a VQA standard.
Datatypestring
Description

The non_clinical_specificity variable records response to the prompt, "The proportion of system-based negatives; calculated as 100xTN/(TN+FP). Used for comparison with a VQA standard.".
817) non_clinical_specificity_ci_percent ¶
LabelPercent used to define two-sided confidence interval for non_clinical specificity. (Example: 95)
Datatypestring
Description

The non_clinical_specificity_ci_percent variable records response to the prompt, "Percent used to define two-sided confidence interval for non_clinical specificity. (Example: 95)".
818) non_clinical_specificity_ci_min ¶
LabelLower bound of confidence interval for non_clinical specificity.
Datatypestring
Description

The non_clinical_specificity_ci_min variable records response to the prompt, "Lower bound of confidence interval for non_clinical specificity.".
819) non_clinical_specificity_ci_max ¶
LabelUpper bound of confidence interval for non_clinical specificity.
Datatypestring
Description

The non_clinical_specificity_ci_max variable records response to the prompt, "Upper bound of confidence interval for non_clinical specificity.".
823) analytical_sensitivity ¶
LabelThe lowest concentration of an analyte that can be detected as positive outcome by the test system 95% of the time. (Example: 19 out of 20 positive sample are detected as positive in an LOD panel.)
Datatypestring
Description

The analytical_sensitivity variable records response to the prompt, "The lowest concentration of an analyte that can be detected as positive outcome by the test system 95% of the time. (Example: 19 out of 20 positive sample are detected as positive in an LOD panel.)".
824) analytical_sensitivity_unit ¶
LabelThe unit of analytical_sensitivity (Example: 19 out of 20 positive sample are detected as positive in an LOD panel.)
Datatypestring
Description

The analytical_sensitivity_unit variable records response to the prompt, "The unit of analytical_sensitivity (Example: 19 out of 20 positive sample are detected as positive in an LOD panel.)".
825) slope_calibration_of_curve ¶
LabelThe slope of the calibration curve. (Armbruster DA, Pry T. Limit of blank, limit of detection and limit of quantitation. Clin Biochem Rev. 2008 Aug;29 Suppl 1(Suppl 1):S49-52. PMID: 18852857; PMCID: PMC2556583; https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8Pe3k3fJNe8)
Datatypestring
Description

The slope_calibration_of_curve variable records response to the prompt, "The slope of the calibration curve. (Armbruster DA, Pry T. Limit of blank, limit of detection and limit of quantitation. Clin Biochem Rev. 2008 Aug;29 Suppl 1(Suppl 1):S49-52. PMID: 18852857; PMCID: PMC2556583; https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8Pe3k3fJNe8)".
826) slope_calibration_of_curve_unit ¶
LabelUnit of slope_calibration_of_curve
Datatypestring
Description

The slope_calibration_of_curve_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of slope_calibration_of_curve".
832) analytical_inclusivity ¶
LabelImpact of variants/mutations on test results. Specificity with respect to variants/mutations compared to wildtype (Analysis can be in silico and wet testing)
Datatypestring
Description

The analytical_inclusivity variable records response to the prompt, "Impact of variants/mutations on test results. Specificity with respect to variants/mutations compared to wildtype (Analysis can be in silico and wet testing)".
833) cross_reactivity_organism_name ¶
LabelName of organism tested for cross-reactivity against target organism (Example: Human coronavirus OC43)
Datatypestring
Description

The cross_reactivity_organism_name variable records response to the prompt, "Name of organism tested for cross-reactivity against target organism (Example: Human coronavirus OC43)".
834) cross_reactivity_organism_taxonomy_id ¶
LabelNCBI taxonomy id of organism tested for cross-reactivity against target organism (Example: 31631)
Datatypestring
Description

The cross_reactivity_organism_taxonomy_id variable records response to the prompt, "NCBI taxonomy id of organism tested for cross-reactivity against target organism (Example: 31631)".
835) cross_reactivity_organism_concentation ¶
LabelConcentration of cross-reacting organism.
Datatypestring
Description

The cross_reactivity_organism_concentation variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration of cross-reacting organism.".
836) cross_reactivity_organism_concentation_unit ¶
LabelConcentation unit used for cross-reacting organism. (Example: copies/ml)
Datatypestring
Description

The cross_reactivity_organism_concentation_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Concentation unit used for cross-reacting organism. (Example: copies/ml)".
837) cross_reactivity_result ¶
LabelResult of cross-reactivity test against target organism. A positive results indicates that cross-reacting organism gives a positive signal.
Datatypestring
Description

The cross_reactivity_result variable records response to the prompt, "Result of cross-reactivity test against target organism. A positive results indicates that cross-reacting organism gives a positive signal.".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 3 permissible string values.

Enumeration
positive positive
negative negative
inconclusive inconclusive
838) cross_reactivity_detail ¶
LabelDescription of observed cross-reactivity.
Datatypestring
Description

The cross_reactivity_detail variable records response to the prompt, "Description of observed cross-reactivity.".
839) interfering_substance ¶
LabelName of the interfering substance. (Example: Menthol)
Datatypestring
Description

The interfering_substance variable records response to the prompt, "Name of the interfering substance. (Example: Menthol)".
840) interfering_substance_provider ¶
LabelName of the entity that provided the interfering substance. (Example: Sigma Aldrich)
Datatypestring
Description

The interfering_substance_provider variable records response to the prompt, "Name of the entity that provided the interfering substance. (Example: Sigma Aldrich)".
841) interfering_substance_concentration ¶
LabelConcentration of the interfering substance. (Example: 1.5)
Datatypestring
Description

The interfering_substance_concentration variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration of the interfering substance. (Example: 1.5)".
842) interfering_substance_concentation_unit ¶
LabelConcentration unit used for interfering substance. (Examples: mg/ml, %v/v)
Datatypestring
Description

The interfering_substance_concentation_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Concentration unit used for interfering substance. (Examples: mg/ml, %v/v)".
843) interfering_substance_detail ¶
LabelDescription of observed interference. (Example: There was no interference of SARS-CoV-2 detection observed from any of the substances tested except for bleach which was shown to interfere at the initial concentration of 1% v/v tested. Further testing indicated that the highest bleach level the test system could tolerate was 0.2% v/v. Source: https://www.fda.gov/media/144592/download.)
Datatypestring
Description

The interfering_substance_detail variable records response to the prompt, "Description of observed interference. (Example: There was no interference of SARS-CoV-2 detection observed from any of the substances tested except for bleach which was shown to interfere at the initial concentration of 1% v/v tested. Further testing indicated that the highest bleach level the test system could tolerate was 0.2% v/v. Source: https://www.fda.gov/media/144592/download.)".

Analytical/Clinical Performance21 elements

820) area_under_the_roc_curve ¶
LabelROC-based performance. AUC under ROC curve.
Datatypestring
Description

The area_under_the_roc_curve variable records response to the prompt, "ROC-based performance. AUC under ROC curve.".
821) roc_x_values ¶
LabelList of false positive rate values (1-specificity)
Datatypestring
Description

The roc_x_values variable records response to the prompt, "List of false positive rate values (1-specificity)".
822) roc_y_values ¶
LabelList of true positive rate values (sensitivity)
Datatypestring
Description

The roc_y_values variable records response to the prompt, "List of true positive rate values (sensitivity)".
844) positive_percent_agreement ¶
LabelPositive Percent Agreement (PPA). The proportion of non-reference standard positive subjects in whom the new test is positive.
Datatypestring
Description

The positive_percent_agreement variable records response to the prompt, "Positive Percent Agreement (PPA). The proportion of non-reference standard positive subjects in whom the new test is positive.".
845) negative_percent_agreement ¶
LabelNegative Percent Agreement (NPA). The proportion of non-reference standard negative subjects in whom the new test is negative.
Datatypestring
Description

The negative_percent_agreement variable records response to the prompt, "Negative Percent Agreement (NPA). The proportion of non-reference standard negative subjects in whom the new test is negative.".
846) overall_percent_agreement ¶
LabelPercentage of total subjects where the new test and the non-reference standard agree.
Datatypestring
Description

The overall_percent_agreement variable records response to the prompt, "Percentage of total subjects where the new test and the non-reference standard agree.".
852) true_label ¶
LabelThe expected label based on a gold standard
Datatypestring
Description

The true_label variable records response to the prompt, "The expected label based on a gold standard".

Values for this variable are strings that are restricted to the list of 3 permissible string values.

Enumeration
positive positive
negative negative
inconclusive inconclusive
853) positive_samples ¶
LabelTotal number of positive samples
Datatypestring
Description

The positive_samples variable records response to the prompt, "Total number of positive samples".
854) negative_samples ¶
LabelTotal number of negative samples
Datatypestring
Description

The negative_samples variable records response to the prompt, "Total number of negative samples".
855) positive_samples_in_calculation ¶
LabelTotal number of positive samples used for the calculation and modeling.
Datatypestring
Description

The positive_samples_in_calculation variable records response to the prompt, "Total number of positive samples used for the calculation and modeling.".
856) negative_samples_in_calculation ¶
LabelTotal number of negative samples used for the calculation and modeling.
Datatypestring
Description

The negative_samples_in_calculation variable records response to the prompt, "Total number of negative samples used for the calculation and modeling.".
857) true_positives ¶
LabelTrue Positives (TP). The number of subjects/specimens with true positive test results
Datatypestring
Description

The true_positives variable records response to the prompt, "True Positives (TP). The number of subjects/specimens with true positive test results".
858) false_positives ¶
LabelFalse Positives (FP). The number of subjects/specimens with false positive test results
Datatypestring
Description

The false_positives variable records response to the prompt, "False Positives (FP). The number of subjects/specimens with false positive test results".
859) true_negatives ¶
LabelTrue Negatives (TN). The number of subjects/specimens with true negative test results.
Datatypestring
Description

The true_negatives variable records response to the prompt, "True Negatives (TN). The number of subjects/specimens with true negative test results.".
860) false_negatives ¶
LabelFalse Negatives (FN). The number of subjects/specimens with false negative test results
Datatypestring
Description

The false_negatives variable records response to the prompt, "False Negatives (FN). The number of subjects/specimens with false negative test results".
882) invalid_test_rate ¶
LabelThe fraction of tests that gave an invalid or undetermined results and where excluded from the calculation of metrics (example: 0.05)
Datatypestring
Description

The invalid_test_rate variable records response to the prompt, "The fraction of tests that gave an invalid or undetermined results and where excluded from the calculation of metrics (example: 0.05)".
883) invalid_test_detail ¶
LabelDescription why test results where excluded from the calculation of metrics
Datatypestring
Description

The invalid_test_detail variable records response to the prompt, "Description why test results where excluded from the calculation of metrics".
884) turnaround_time ¶
LabelTurnaround time for test in minutes
Datatypestring
Description

The turnaround_time variable records response to the prompt, "Turnaround time for test in minutes".
885) turnaround_time_unit ¶
LabelUnit for turnaround_time
Datatypestring
Description

The turnaround_time_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit for turnaround_time".
887) validation_test ¶
LabelType of cross validation used for the statistical method used
Datatypestring
Description

The validation_test variable records response to the prompt, "Type of cross validation used for the statistical method used".
888) validation_test_permutations ¶
LabelNumber of permutations performed for the cross validation of the statistical method used.
Datatypestring
Description

The validation_test_permutations variable records response to the prompt, "Number of permutations performed for the cross validation of the statistical method used.".

Clinical Performance32 elements

827) analytical_cutoff_description ¶
LabelDescription of the analytical cutoff
Datatypestring
Description

The analytical_cutoff_description variable records response to the prompt, "Description of the analytical cutoff".
828) analytical_cutoff_min ¶
LabelTest is considered positive if assay readout is geater than or equal the analytical_cutoff_min value
Datatypestring
Description

The analytical_cutoff_min variable records response to the prompt, "Test is considered positive if assay readout is geater than or equal the analytical_cutoff_min value".
829) analytical_cutoff_min_unit ¶
LabelUnit of analytical_cutoff_min
Datatypestring
Description

The analytical_cutoff_min_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of analytical_cutoff_min".
830) analytical_cutoff_max ¶
LabelTest is considered positive if assay readout is less than or equal the analytical_cutoff_max value
Datatypestring
Description

The analytical_cutoff_max variable records response to the prompt, "Test is considered positive if assay readout is less than or equal the analytical_cutoff_max value".
831) analytical_cutoff_max_unit ¶
LabelUnit of analytical_cutoff_max
Datatypestring
Description

The analytical_cutoff_max_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of analytical_cutoff_max".
847) target_condition ¶
LabelA particular disease, a disease stage, health status, or any other identifiable condition within a patient, such as staging a disease already known to be present, or a health condition that should prompt clinical action, such as the initiation, modification, or termination of treatment.
Datatypestring
Description

The target_condition variable records response to the prompt, "A particular disease, a disease stage, health status, or any other identifiable condition within a patient, such as staging a disease already known to be present, or a health condition that should prompt clinical action, such as the initiation, modification, or termination of treatment.".
848) reference_standard ¶
LabelThe best available method for establishing the presence or absence of the target condition; the reference standard can be a single test or method, or a combination of methods and techniques, including clinical follow-up.
Datatypestring
Description

The reference_standard variable records response to the prompt, "The best available method for establishing the presence or absence of the target condition; the reference standard can be a single test or method, or a combination of methods and techniques, including clinical follow-up.".
849) study_population ¶
LabelThe subjects/patients (and specimen types) included in the study
Datatypestring
Description

The study_population variable records response to the prompt, "The subjects/patients (and specimen types) included in the study".
850) stratification ¶
LabelStratification of subjects, e.g., days since onset of symptoms, asymptomatic vs. symptomatic, co-infections, PCR Cycle Threshold, n x LOD concentration ranges for contrived samples
Datatypestring
Description

The stratification variable records response to the prompt, "Stratification of subjects, e.g., days since onset of symptoms, asymptomatic vs. symptomatic, co-infections, PCR Cycle Threshold, n x LOD concentration ranges for contrived samples".
851) cohort_size ¶
LabelNumber of subject in a cohort (see cohort_id) used to calculate metrics
Datatypestring
Description

The cohort_size variable records response to the prompt, "Number of subject in a cohort (see cohort_id) used to calculate metrics".
861) clinical_sensitivity ¶
LabelThe proportion of subjects with the target condition in whom the test is positive; calculated as 100xTP/(TP+FN). Used for comparison with a reference (Gold) standard. (PCR is Gold standard for COVID-19. For comparison with a non-reference standard use percent agreement metrics.)
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_sensitivity variable records response to the prompt, "The proportion of subjects with the target condition in whom the test is positive; calculated as 100xTP/(TP+FN). Used for comparison with a reference (Gold) standard. (PCR is Gold standard for COVID-19. For comparison with a non-reference standard use percent agreement metrics.)".
862) clinical_sensitivity_ci_percent ¶
LabelPercent used to define two-sided confidence interval for clinical sensitivity. (Example: 95)
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_sensitivity_ci_percent variable records response to the prompt, "Percent used to define two-sided confidence interval for clinical sensitivity. (Example: 95)".
863) clinical_sensitivity_ci_min ¶
LabelLower bound of confidence interval for clinical sensitivity.
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_sensitivity_ci_min variable records response to the prompt, "Lower bound of confidence interval for clinical sensitivity.".
864) clinical_sensitivity_ci_max ¶
LabelUpper bound of confidence interval for clinical sensitivity.
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_sensitivity_ci_max variable records response to the prompt, "Upper bound of confidence interval for clinical sensitivity.".
865) clinical_specificity ¶
LabelThe proportion of subjects without the target condition in whom the test is negative; calculated as 100xTN/(FP+TN). (PCR is Gold standard for COVID-19. For comparison with a non-reference standard use percent agreement metrics.)
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_specificity variable records response to the prompt, "The proportion of subjects without the target condition in whom the test is negative; calculated as 100xTN/(FP+TN). (PCR is Gold standard for COVID-19. For comparison with a non-reference standard use percent agreement metrics.)".
866) clinical_specificity_ci_percent ¶
LabelPercent used to define two-sided confidence interval for clinical specificity. (Example: 95)
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_specificity_ci_percent variable records response to the prompt, "Percent used to define two-sided confidence interval for clinical specificity. (Example: 95)".
867) clinical_specificity_ci_min ¶
LabelLower bound of confidence interval for clinical specificity.
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_specificity_ci_min variable records response to the prompt, "Lower bound of confidence interval for clinical specificity.".
868) clinical_specificity_ci_max ¶
LabelUpper bound of confidence interval for clinical specificity.
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_specificity_ci_max variable records response to the prompt, "Upper bound of confidence interval for clinical specificity.".
869) positive_predictive_value ¶
LabelThe proportion of test positive patients who have the target condition; calculated as 100xTP/(TP+FP)
Datatypestring
Description

The positive_predictive_value variable records response to the prompt, "The proportion of test positive patients who have the target condition; calculated as 100xTP/(TP+FP)".
870) positive_predictive_value_ci_percent ¶
LabelPercent used to define two-sided confidence interval for positive predicted value. (Example: 95)
Datatypestring
Description

The positive_predictive_value_ci_percent variable records response to the prompt, "Percent used to define two-sided confidence interval for positive predicted value. (Example: 95)".
871) positive_predictive_value_ci_min ¶
LabelLower bound of confidence interval for positive predicted value
Datatypestring
Description

The positive_predictive_value_ci_min variable records response to the prompt, "Lower bound of confidence interval for positive predicted value".
872) positive_predictive_value_ci_max ¶
LabelUpper bound of confidence interval for positive predicted value
Datatypestring
Description

The positive_predictive_value_ci_max variable records response to the prompt, "Upper bound of confidence interval for positive predicted value".
873) negative_predictive_value ¶
LabelThe proportion of test negative patients who do not have the target condition; calculated as 100xTN/(TN+FN)
Datatypestring
Description

The negative_predictive_value variable records response to the prompt, "The proportion of test negative patients who do not have the target condition; calculated as 100xTN/(TN+FN)".
874) negative_predictive_value_ci_percent ¶
LabelPercent used to define two-sided confidence interval for negative predicted value. (Example: 95)
Datatypestring
Description

The negative_predictive_value_ci_percent variable records response to the prompt, "Percent used to define two-sided confidence interval for negative predicted value. (Example: 95)".
875) negative_predictive_value_ci_min ¶
LabelLower bound of confidence interval or negative predicted value
Datatypestring
Description

The negative_predictive_value_ci_min variable records response to the prompt, "Lower bound of confidence interval or negative predicted value".
876) negative_predictive_value_ci_max ¶
LabelUpper bound of confidence interval or negative predicted value
Datatypestring
Description

The negative_predictive_value_ci_max variable records response to the prompt, "Upper bound of confidence interval or negative predicted value".
877) clinical_cutoff_description ¶
LabelDescription of the clinical cutoff
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_cutoff_description variable records response to the prompt, "Description of the clinical cutoff".
878) clinical_cutoff_min ¶
LabelTest is considered positive if assay readout is greater than or equal the clinical_cutoff_min value
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_cutoff_min variable records response to the prompt, "Test is considered positive if assay readout is greater than or equal the clinical_cutoff_min value".
879) clinical_cutoff_min_unit ¶
LabelUnit of clinical_cutoff_min
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_cutoff_min_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of clinical_cutoff_min".
880) clinical_cutoff_max ¶
LabelTest is considered positive if assay readout is less than or equal the clinical_cutoff_max value
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_cutoff_max variable records response to the prompt, "Test is considered positive if assay readout is less than or equal the clinical_cutoff_max value".
881) clinical_cutoff_max_unit ¶
LabelUnit of clinical_cutoff_max
Datatypestring
Description

The clinical_cutoff_max_unit variable records response to the prompt, "Unit of clinical_cutoff_max".
886) price_per_test ¶
LabelPrice per test in USD
Datatypestring
Description

The price_per_test variable records response to the prompt, "Price per test in USD".
